title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: AWS Power Hour: Generative AI For Developers | S1 E1 | Exam Overview and FM Integrations

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Hosts break down the exam format, share study strategies, and guide listeners through designing resilient GenAI architectures. The episode covers building data processing pipelines for foundation models, implementing vector stores for RAG systems, applying prompt engineering techniques, and analyzing sample exam questions alongside real-world solutions for advanced GenAI systems.
bullets: Overview of exam format and recommended study strategies.
Guidance on designing resilient generative AI architectures.
How to build data processing pipelines tailored to foundation models.
Implementing vector stores for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
Prompt engineering tips and analysis of sample exam questions with real-world solutions.
takeaways: Ensure infrastructure can support high-throughput data pipelines required by foundation models.
Provision low-latency storage and compute for vector search and RAG workloads.
Design for resilience and scalability to handle production GenAI demands and minimize downtime.
Plan capacity, networking, and operational procedures to support advanced model deployment and maintenance.

items.title: re:Invent - Chalk Talk - Solving private IPv4 exhaustion using AWS Cloud WAN & Amazon VPC Lattice

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this session addresses private IPv4 exhaustion caused by limited address planning, architectural shifts, mergers, and IP-hungry technologies like containers. It outlines a scalable, cost-effective approach using AWS Cloud WAN with service insertion and Amazon VPC Lattice to centralize private NAT Gateways and AWS PrivateLink, aiming to maximize IP utilization, reduce costs, and simplify network management across distributed environments.
bullets: Problem: private IPv4 exhaustion from planning limits, mergers, and container adoption.
Solution stack: AWS Cloud WAN with service insertion plus Amazon VPC Lattice.
Centralizes private NAT Gateways and AWS PrivateLink to conserve IP space.
Expected benefits: better IP utilization, cost reduction, simplified network ops.
Designed to support evolving infrastructure across distributed environments.
takeaways: Centralizing NAT and PrivateLink can stretch existing private IPv4 pools and delay or avoid renumbering.
Combining Cloud WAN and VPC Lattice provides a scalable, repeatable architecture for distributed networks.
Reduced address footprint and centralized management can lower operational costs and speed new project onboarding.
This approach offers a durable option to manage IP scarcity as infrastructure and container usage grow.

items.title: Evan Bates Praises Figure Skating's Next Generation

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Evan Bates shares why the future of figure skating has never looked brighter. The short Team USA clip emphasizes a positive outlook for the sport and directs viewers to Team USA’s YouTube and social channels.
bullets: No transcript is available for this video.
Evan Bates praises the next generation of figure skaters, saying the future looks brighter.
Video is tagged with #WinterOlympics.
Description includes calls-to-action and links to Team USA’s YouTube and multiple social platforms.
takeaways: Olympic-related, positive athlete content can drive audience interest—monitor streaming and CDN capacity during peak Olympic coverage windows.
Ensure video hosting and social delivery infrastructure is prepared for potential spikes tied to athlete-driven clips and calls-to-action.
Track engagement from social links and hashtags (e.g., #WinterOlympics) to inform short-term scaling and caching strategies.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, full-time contractor Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre property in upstate New York in 2015 with the goal of building Airbnb properties. He commuted on weekends from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin himself, added more cabins over time, and by September 2025 the property had generated over $2.1 million.
bullets: 2015 purchase of a five-acre plot in upstate New York to develop short-term rentals.
Chris Broomfield, a full-time contractor, made weekend commutes from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin himself over three years.
Additional cabins were constructed on the property after the initial build.
By September 2025 the property had produced over $2.1 million in revenue.
takeaways: A single, owner-driven short-term rental project can scale into a multimillion-dollar asset over several years, signaling potential sustained local demand.
Rural hospitality developments concentrated on one site may create ongoing infrastructure and service needs that warrant planning.
Phased, incremental construction by owner-operators can lead to variable timing of demand on local utilities and connectivity.
Early, low-cost land investment can enable expansion of on-site assets, which has implications for long-term regional capacity and provisioning.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The episode explains how operational changes—such as moving production, distribution, teams or contracts—are evaluated as restructurings from the perspective of transfer pricing and valuations, what reporting obligations (TPR, MDR) may arise, and why preparing documentation on time reduces future risk. KPMG experts Monika Palmowska and Tomasz Regulski cover how to recognize significant changes (including EBIT analysis), common valuation errors, and provide a practical checklist for firms.
bullets: Defines restructuring in transfer-pricing terms and how to spot it.
Explains how to determine material change using EBIT analysis.
Stresses the importance of timely restructuring documentation.
Summarizes reporting obligations, notably TPR and MDR requirements.
Covers valuation roles, common mistakes, employee/contract transfers, and a practical checklist.
takeaways: If moving workloads, assets, or teams, assess transfer-pricing impact early to avoid later disputes.
Prepare and retain timely documentation and valuations to meet TPR/MDR obligations and reduce audit risk.
Include EBIT-impact analysis when judging whether an operational change is material.
Evaluate employee and contract transfers as part of the restructuring assessment to spot hidden tax/reporting consequences.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Marcus Oliver (lead of Google Delta, Google’s professional services team) says the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a strategy — not compute costs. He advises businesses to reimagine value chains and deploy multi-agent systems to drive real business outcomes.
bullets: The biggest AI mistake is jumping into code without a clear strategy.
The costliest risk is strategic, not primarily computing expenses.
Focus on reimagining value chains rather than immediate implementation.
Use multi-agent systems as a means to achieve real business outcomes.
Engage professional services (Google Delta) to help avoid the 'coding trap'.
takeaways: Prioritize strategic planning and solution design before committing infrastructure spend.
Don’t treat compute cost as the main risk—align AI projects to business value first.
Assess how reimagined value chains will change infrastructure and service needs.
Plan to support multi-agent system deployments and involve professional services for guidance.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description, DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform designed to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions by providing visibility and protection against evolving threats. It highlights three core capabilities—AI Lens for visibility and policy enforcement, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing, and an AI Firewall for real-time prompt and response guardrails.
bullets: Unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions.
AI Lens: visibility and policy application across user–AI interactions.
AI Red Teaming: stress-test applications and models against real-world attacks.
AI Firewall: real-time protection of prompts and responses with built-in guardrails.
Aimed at eliminating shadow AI and protecting infrastructure from evolving threats.
takeaways: Centralize AI security to gain visibility and reduce unmanaged (shadow) AI usage.
Use red teaming to identify real-world attack vectors before they impact operations.
Apply real-time guardrails (AI Firewall) to lower risk from harmful prompts/responses.
A unified approach can help protect infrastructure and enforce policies consistently.
